do_not_copy: Do not copy source visuals, layouts, screenshots, brand marks, template files, product UI captures, or deck assets.
observed_move: Reveal the product mechanism through staged editable surfaces rather than explaining every layer at once.
why_it_works: A staged reveal lets the viewer see control forming step by step. It converts process complexity into a sequence of visible choices.
ppt_translation: Construct an editable diagram or shape sequence where each stage has a distinct weight: source data, compiled memory, generation, QA, and release gate.
quality_risk: The reveal turns into a report or dashboard if every step gets equal visual weight and the audience cannot see what changed.

extraction_units.source_anchor: product presentation reference: staged mechanism reveal
extraction_units.derived_rule: Stage source data, memory, code generation, QA, and gate as unequal surfaces based on narrative importance.
extraction_units.execution_guard: Do not give every stage equal box size or identical visual weight.
extraction_units.qa_probe: The proof slide shows a visible hierarchy between source, memory, output, and gate.

extraction_units.source_anchor: product presentation reference: control surface emergence
extraction_units.derived_rule: Make one generator choice visible: what is shown, removed, routed, or gated.
extraction_units.execution_guard: Do not describe all pipeline layers as text-only process boxes.
extraction_units.qa_probe: The viewer can identify at least one design decision made by the skill before reading notes.
